description_long: This radio receiver features a large, rectangular main body with a smooth, beige exterior and a slightly rounded front face. The front panel is accented with a faux wood strip, housing three prominent rotary knobs, a circular analog dial, and two rectangular speaker grilles. On the left side, a large, disc-shaped component is attached, possibly representing a tuning or signal element, while the right side displays two vertical, ribbed antenna structures mounted on white bases. The top and sides are plain, with the rear showing a small window revealing internal gears and mechanisms. The overall design is retro-inspired, evoking mid-20th-century radio aesthetics with a blend of plastic and wood-like materials. The object is compact and likely intended for tabletop or shelf placement in an urban environment.
description: A retro-style radio receiver with a beige body, wood-accented front panel, rotary knobs, analog dial, and visible antennas. Features a large disc on one side and a small window showing internal gears.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main face with three rotary knobs, a circular dial, two speaker grilles, and the large disc and antenna structures on either side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the plain back and the two vertical antenna structures, with no visible controls or dials.
description_view_2: Top view displaying the flat beige surface and a small window revealing internal gears, with the disc and antennas visible at the edges.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the large disc-shaped component and the coiled wire, with the plain beige body dominating the view.
